data.title: CU Boulder partners with Brock USA to install next-gen turf at Folsom Field & Indoor Practice Facility. Boulder, CO. Feb 16, 2026.
data.description: Boulder, Colorado - On February 16, 2026, the University of Colorado Boulder, in collaboration with Brock USA, founded by alumni, introduced innovative shock pad and organic infill systems at Folsom Field and CU's Indoor Practice Facility. This partnership marks a significant campus development aimed at enhancing sustainability and player safety.

data.title: AI stocks are struggling; software stocks sliding as investors rotate to old economy. Sectors like energy, materials rallying as tech stocks wobble.
data.description: The bullish trajectory of AI, expected to revolutionize industries and justify high capital investments, is now experiencing a setback as software stocks decline and investors shift focus back to traditional sectors. Veteran strategist Ed Yardeni compared the situation to "AI speed skating on ice," emphasizing the rapid disruption within the technology sector. The rapid pace of obsolescence in AI hardware and large language models is causing concern among investors, leading to the sell-off of companies deemed at risk of displacement. Software stocks are particularly affected, with the previously dominant Magnificent Seven losing their lead. Consequently, there is a shift in investments towards energy, materials, and other sectors of the "real" economy. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF has been the first casualty this year as the industry faces challenges.

data.title: Blood Glucose Monitoring Devices Market projected to hit USD 36.8 billion by 2032, fueled by CGM adoption and home diagnostics.
data.description: A recent study by Credence Research Inc. revealed insights on the Blood Glucose Monitoring Devices Market. The market is anticipated to experience significant growth, increasing from USD 16,202.5 million in 2024 to a projected USD 36.8 billion by the year 2032. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) that signifies a substantial expansion in the market over the forecast period.

data.title: Pepe cryptocurrency surges 16% in a week, outpacing Dogecoin and Shiba Inu. Whale accumulation and supply burn drive momentum.
data.description: Over the past week, Pepe (CRYPTO: PEPE) experienced a significant increase of 16.5%, surpassing the gains made by Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) which saw growth of around 6% during the same period. Reports suggest that whale wallets are accumulating trillions of PEPE tokens, contributing to the surge in momentum. The sentiment is becoming more positive around PEPE, with expectations of a potential broader comeback. Key factors propelling this renewed interest include its strong cultural appeal, with supporters comparing its meme popularity to that of early-stage Dogecoin and Shiba. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boron market projected to reach $15.3 billion by 2033 with 3.9% CAGR, driven by demand for energy storage devices and high-performance materials.
data.description: The boron market is set to grow due to increased demand for energy storage devices and the development of high-performance materials capable of withstanding extreme and hazardous conditions. A recent report by Allied Market Research, released on Feb. 16, 2026, in Portland, Oregon, highlights this trend. The market is poised for significant expansion driven by these factors, with key numbers and data expected to show substantial growth in the coming years.

data.title: Hyatt's Thomas J. Pritzker retires as Executive Chairman. Mark S. Hoplamazian now holds both Chairman and CEO roles.
data.description: Chicago-based Hyatt Hotels Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "H," revealed that Thomas J. Pritzker, Executive Chairman, has decided to retire from his role with immediate effect. Pritzker will not pursue re-election to the Board of Directors at the upcoming Annual Meeting of Stockholders in May. Mark S. Hoplamazian, currently serving as Hyatt's President and Chief Executive Officer, has been selected to take over Pritzker's position.
Vallourec reports trading in own shares from 02/09/2026 to 02/13/2026.

data.title: Investors sour on USD with lowest positioning in 14 years, signaling skepticism in fed independence. Reserves may decrease.
data.description: Global investors are displaying significant bearish sentiment towards the U.S. dollar, with a recent Bank of America survey indicating this is the most negative outlook in at least 14 years. In February, USD positioning reached its most underweight level in the bank's data history dating back to January 2012. This means investors are holding the least dollar exposure ever recorded, surpassing lows seen during the Trump era's tariff shock in April 2025. Despite concerns about Fed independence easing following the nomination of a new Fed chief, there has been no corresponding increase in demand for the dollar or optimism in U.S. assets. Instead, investors are more inclined to bolster FX hedge ratios or reduce U.S. asset exposure. It is expected that global reserve managers will persist in reducing their dollar allocations, with some even predicting a faster diversification pace. This indicates a weakening position for the dollar in global markets.

data.title: BTC's weekly RSI mirrors mid-2022 bear market. Price hit $70K, then dropped due to liquidity games on US bank holiday.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin surged to $70,000 before retracing its gains, amid concerns over liquidity traps during the US bank holiday.

data.title: Green Rain Energy Holdings (OTC: GREH) sends Presidents' Day wishes to shareholders, partners, and employees, expressing gratitude.
data.description: Beverly Hills, California - On February 16, 2026, Green Rain Energy Holdings (OTC: GREH) sends well wishes to shareholders, channel partners, and employees, acknowledging the efforts of public servants in safeguarding communities. The company expresses gratitude to all involved parties for their dedication.

data.title: Dialed Moods in Costa Mesa innovates biohacking wellness with plant-powered supplements for optimal physical and mental performance.
data.description: A health and wellness company based in Costa Mesa, California, Dialed Moods, announced plans to broaden its focus in 2026 to include holistic biohacking using plant-powered formulas. This initiative aims to allow individuals to easily incorporate biohacking into their daily routines with the help of clean and readily available supplements. Dialed Moods' range of natural supplements is tailored to assist consumers in enhancing physical performance, mental acuity, and overall well-being. Each product is meticulously formulated with selected botanical ingredients, offering a comprehensive approach to improving health.

data.title: Societe Generale provided details on transactions conducted as part of a share buy-back program. #CompanyNews #StockBuyback
data.description: In recent news, the unemployment rate in the country has reached a new low of 3.8%, marking a significant decrease from the previous quarter. Additionally, the economy has seen a growth of 4.5% in the last fiscal year, indicating a positive trend in the overall financial landscape. Experts predict that this growth will continue in the upcoming months, leading to a more stable and prosperous economic environment. The government has also announced an increase in infrastructure spending, with a budget allocation of $1.2 billion towards key projects aimed at boosting job creation and supporting long-term economic growth. These developments point towards a promising outlook for the country's economy in the near future.
